In Russian many verbs change their ending

Now here is something really cool about one of today's words.
Want to know a secret about volk?
The Russian word volk and the English word wolf are actually the exact same ancient
They both come from the same roots spoken more than 5,000 years ago.
This word traveled through different languages.
It became wolf in English and volk in Russian.
The pronunciation changed just a little.
English uses W, but Russian uses strong V.
So volk and wolf are like twins separated at birth.
Next time you see the word wolf, remember its cousin volk.
